prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a blix has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the blix-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a blix, a woz, and a moob. The blix is underneath the woz. The woz is to-the-left-of the moob. The blix has a green button on top, a yellow button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The woz has a yellow button on top and a green button on bottom. The moob has a purple button on top and a green button on side. Pressing the green button on the blix activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a vask, a vro, and a glim. The vro is underneath the glim. The glim is to-the-left-of the vask. The vask has a purple button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The vro has a green button on top, a yellow button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The glim has a green button on top, a green button on side, and a yellow button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the blix-analog, and which button activates it?
